metadata.note: Output used as-is without manual revision or selection.

causal_texts: The glass slipped from Alice's hand. The glass fell to the floor. The glass broke into many pieces. The glass left a wet stain on the tile floor.

The tall candle burned steadily on the wooden dinner table. The tall candle dripped soft wax onto the white linen cloth. The tall candle grew shorter through the long quiet evening. The tall candle went out in a thin curl of gray smoke.

Maria chopped the fresh vegetables on a small cutting board. Maria dropped the fresh vegetables into a pot of boiling water. Maria stirred the hot soup with a long wooden spoon. Maria poured the hot soup into two deep ceramic bowls.

The snowman stood proudly in the middle of the front yard. The snowman softened under the warm rays of the bright sun. The snowman lost the carrot nose and the round coal eyes. The snowman melted into a wide puddle on the green grass.

Tom wrote the long letter at the quiet kitchen table. Tom folded the long letter into the clean white envelope. Tom carried the clean white envelope to the corner mailbox. Tom pushed the clean white envelope through the narrow metal slot.

The paper boat set off from the muddy edge of the garden pond. The paper boat drifted toward the reeds on a light breeze. The paper boat soaked up cold water through the folded hull. The paper boat sank slowly beside a large flat stone.

The old oak tree swayed hard in the rising autumn wind. The old oak tree cracked loudly at the base of the trunk. The old oak tree crashed across the narrow country road. The old oak tree blocked the narrow country road for a full day.

Priya planted the sunflower seeds in a sunny garden bed. Priya watered the sunny garden bed through a long dry spell. Priya tied the tall green stems to a bamboo stake. Priya cut the golden flower heads for a kitchen vase.

The copper kettle heated slowly on the small gas stove. The copper kettle rattled the metal lid with rising steam. The copper kettle whistled a sharp note through the quiet kitchen. The copper kettle filled the round teapot with boiling water.

Kenji noticed a slow leak in the rear tire of the blue bicycle. Kenji patched the rear tire with a small rubber kit. Kenji pumped the rear tire firm with a silver hand pump. Kenji rode the blue bicycle down the lane without a wobble.

The brown river swelled after days of heavy mountain rain. The brown river spilled over the low grassy banks at the bend. The brown river flooded the empty fields beside the mill. The brown river left thick mud across the narrow towpath.

Amara mixed the soft dough in the big ceramic bowl. Amara kneaded the soft dough on the floured kitchen counter. Amara baked the soft dough in the hot brick oven. Amara pulled a golden loaf out of the hot brick oven.

The storm cloud gathered above the wide green valley. The storm cloud darkened the sky over the small farms. The storm cloud released heavy rain onto the thirsty fields. The storm cloud drifted away beyond the low eastern hills.

Leo laced up the sturdy boots at the trailhead. Leo climbed the steep path through the pine forest. Leo reached the rocky summit above the clouds. Leo photographed the wide valley from the rocky summit.

The ice cube dropped into the tall glass of lemonade. The ice cube bobbed against the rim of the tall glass. The ice cube shrank in the warm summer air. The ice cube vanished into the cold cloudy drink.

The green apple broke free from the highest branch. The green apple bounced off the mossy roots below. The green apple rolled down the gentle grassy slope. The green apple came to rest against the garden wall.

Sofia filled the metal watering can at the outdoor tap. Sofia carried the metal watering can across the sunny yard. Sofia watered the thirsty roses along the wooden fence. Sofia emptied the last drops onto the potted mint.

The model rocket ignited on the small launch pad. The model rocket shot upward with a sharp hiss. The model rocket released a bright orange parachute at the peak. The model rocket floated down into the mowed field.

The garden spider spun a wide web between two fence posts. The garden spider waited at the center of the wide web. The garden spider felt a sudden tug from a trapped fly. The garden spider wrapped the trapped fly in fine silk.

The bathtub filled slowly with warm soapy water. The bathtub reached the line of the overflow drain. The bathtub spilled a thin sheet of water over the rim. The bathtub soaked the small rug on the bathroom floor.

Daniel cleared a flat patch of ground near the tall pines. Daniel spread the canvas tent across the flat patch. Daniel staked the four corners with a heavy mallet. Daniel crawled into the canvas tent with a warm sleeping bag.
The tiny seed sank into the dark loose soil. The tiny seed soaked up the gentle spring rain. The tiny seed split open beneath the warm ground. The tiny seed pushed a pale green shoot into the light.

The night train rolled out of the quiet country station. The night train crossed the long iron bridge over the gorge. The night train climbed the steep grade through the dark forest. The night train arrived at the small mountain town.

Nina wound the blue wool into a tidy ball. Nina knitted row upon row beside the warm stove. Nina bound off the last stitches of the long scarf. Nina wrapped the long scarf around a cold neck at the door.

The big wave rose far out beyond the sandbar. The big wave rolled toward the crowded summer beach. The big wave broke into white foam on the shore. The big wave dragged loose shells back toward the deep water.

Omar sprayed the dusty car with the garden hose. Omar scrubbed the dusty car with a soft yellow sponge. Omar rinsed the soapy foam off the shiny paint. Omar dried the shiny paint with a clean cotton towel.

The red balloon slipped out of a small open hand. The red balloon climbed past the rooftops and chimneys. The red balloon drifted over the crowded market square. The red balloon burst on the sharp tip of a weathervane.

The old dishwasher hummed loudly through the rinse cycle. The old dishwasher sprang a slow leak at the door seal. The old dishwasher dripped gray water onto the kitchen tiles. The old dishwasher shut down with a weak final click.

Elena stirred the white paint with a flat wooden stick. Elena brushed a smooth coat onto the weathered fence. Elena added a careful second coat over the thin patches. Elena rinsed the sticky brushes under the outdoor tap.

The paper kite caught a strong gust above the open field. The paper kite climbed high over the row of poplars. The paper kite dove in a sudden loop toward the ground. The paper kite tangled in the branches of a bare elm.

The brown bear woke inside the dark winter den. The brown bear lumbered out into the melting snow. The brown bear followed the rushing river to a shallow bend. The brown bear caught a silver fish in the shallow bend.

Hassan shut off the water valve under the sink. Hassan replaced the worn washer inside the dripping faucet. Hassan tightened the metal fittings with a small wrench. Hassan turned the water valve back on without a single drip.

The milk warmed slowly in the small steel pan. The milk rose in a white foam toward the rim. The milk spilled over the edge of the small steel pan. The milk burned into a brown crust on the hot stove.

Grandpa Ray cut a straight branch from the willow by the creek. Grandpa Ray peeled the smooth bark with a pocket knife. Grandpa Ray carved a deep notch near one rounded end. Grandpa Ray blew a clear sweet note across the quiet porch.

The library book toppled from the crowded top shelf. The library book landed open on the dusty wooden floor. The library book lost a loose page near the worn spine. The library book gathered a film of dust beside the reading chair.

Fatima squeezed a dozen ripe lemons over the wide bowl. Fatima stirred sugar into the tart yellow juice. Fatima poured the sweet mixture over cracked ice in the pitcher. Fatima filled four tall glasses for the thirsty painters outside.

The volcano rumbled deep beneath the sleeping village. The volcano spewed a tall column of gray ash. The volcano poured glowing lava down the southern slope. The volcano buried the old goat path under black rock.

The small puppy chased the rubber ball across the lawn. The small puppy caught the rubber ball near the hedge. The small puppy chewed the rubber ball into ragged pieces. The small puppy buried the ragged pieces beside the rose bed.

Marcus felt a hard jolt from the front left tire. Marcus steered the old sedan onto the gravel shoulder. Marcus jacked up the old sedan beside the flat tire. Marcus bolted the spare wheel firmly into place.

The beaver dragged fresh branches across the shallow stream. The beaver stacked the fresh branches into a rough wall. The beaver packed cold mud into every gap of the rough wall. The beaver raised a still pond behind the finished dam.

Aiko cut the bright paper into perfect squares. Aiko folded the perfect squares into small cranes. Aiko strung the small cranes onto a length of red thread. Aiko hung the length of red thread beside the sunny window.

The glacier crept down the wide mountain valley. The glacier ground the gray boulders beneath the heavy ice. The glacier cracked along a deep blue seam near the shore. The glacier dropped a huge iceberg into the cold bay.

Ruth threaded the thin needle with strong black thread. Ruth stitched the loose button onto the wool coat. Ruth knotted the strong black thread on the inside seam. Ruth snipped the trailing end with small sharp scissors.

The lightning bolt flashed above the darkened wheat field. The lightning bolt struck the lone oak on the ridge. The lightning bolt split the thick trunk down the middle. The lightning bolt left a black scar across the pale wood.

Pedro cast the fishing line into the calm green pool. Pedro felt a strong tug on the fishing line. Pedro reeled in a spotted trout with steady turns. Pedro released the spotted trout back into the calm green pool.

The paint can wobbled on the narrow ladder shelf. The paint can tipped over the edge of the narrow ladder shelf. The paint can splashed white paint across the canvas tarp. The paint can rolled empty into the corner of the room.

Lena unpacked the flat cardboard box in the living room. Lena screwed the wooden panels together with a small tool. Lena stood the finished bookshelf against the bare wall. Lena loaded the finished bookshelf with heavy old novels.

The bright meteor streaked across the clear night sky. The bright meteor flared white above the desert hills. The bright meteor broke into a shower of glowing sparks. The bright meteor faded above the dark horizon.
The tide rose across the wide flat beach. The tide lifted the small boats in the muddy harbor. The tide lapped against the base of the stone seawall. The tide turned back toward the open gray sea.

Ibrahim puffed cool smoke gently over the wooden hive. Ibrahim lifted the heavy frames out of the wooden hive. Ibrahim scraped the golden comb into a wide bucket. Ibrahim strained the fresh honey into rows of glass jars.

The avalanche broke loose from the high white cornice. The avalanche roared down the steep open slope. The avalanche buried the marked trail under deep snow. The avalanche settled into a heavy silent field of white.

Chloe slid the blank photo paper into the tray of developer. Chloe watched a faint image darken on the blank photo paper. Chloe rinsed the blank photo paper in the cool water bath. Chloe pinned the finished print on the drying line.

The campfire caught on a handful of dry pine needles. The campfire spread to the ring of stacked logs. The campfire burned down to a bed of orange coals. The campfire faded into a mound of warm gray ash.

Yusuf packed the heavy books into the cardboard cartons. Yusuf taped the cardboard cartons shut with wide brown tape. Yusuf loaded the cardboard cartons into the rental van. Yusuf stacked the cardboard cartons inside the new apartment.

The caterpillar ate a ragged path through the milkweed leaves. The caterpillar spun a pale silk cocoon under a branch. The caterpillar rested hidden inside the pale silk cocoon. The caterpillar broke out of the pale silk cocoon with damp new wings.

Anya spread thick white frosting over the birthday cake. Anya piped small pink roses along the edge of the birthday cake. Anya wrote a short greeting across the top of the birthday cake. Anya packed the birthday cake into a white bakery box.

The water pipe chilled in the hard overnight frost. The water pipe froze solid behind the kitchen wall. The water pipe cracked along a thin hidden seam. The water pipe sprayed cold water across the wooden floorboards.

Diego laced the worn running shoes by the front door. Diego jogged along the dirt trail beside the quiet river. Diego sprinted the last stretch toward the old stone bridge. Diego cooled down on the soft grass near the old stone bridge.

The sandcastle stood tall near the shining water line. The sandcastle met the leading edge of the rising water. The sandcastle crumbled under the push of the white foam. The sandcastle melted into a smooth mound of wet sand.

Ingrid boiled the dark berries with sugar in a copper pot. Ingrid skimmed the pink foam off the bubbling jam. Ingrid ladled the bubbling jam into warm glass jars. Ingrid sealed the warm glass jars with tight metal lids.

The truck engine turned over with a rough dry cough. The truck engine sputtered through a few uneven strokes. The truck engine settled into a steady low rumble. The truck engine pulled the loaded trailer up the muddy lane.

Noor climbed the folding ladder into the dark attic. Noor handed the dusty boxes down through the narrow hatch. Noor swept the bare boards with a stiff straw broom. Noor lowered the folding ladder and shut the narrow hatch.

The tomato hung small and green on the sturdy vine. The tomato blushed with a few pale streaks of orange. The tomato ripened to a deep glossy red in the sun. The tomato dropped softly into the tall garden grass.

Ravi charged the drone battery at the kitchen counter. Ravi launched the small drone above the apple orchard. Ravi filmed the neat rows of trees from high above. Ravi landed the small drone gently on the picnic table.

The ferry left the wooden dock with a low horn blast. The ferry plowed through the choppy gray water of the strait. The ferry slowed beside the breakwater of the little island. The ferry docked at the island pier near the fish market.

Greta sliced the top off the fat orange pumpkin. Greta scooped the stringy seeds into a metal bowl. Greta carved a crooked grin into the fat orange pumpkin. Greta set a lit candle inside the fat orange pumpkin.

The mole tunneled beneath the tidy vegetable patch. The mole pushed a fresh mound of soil into the open air. The mole nibbled the tender roots of the young carrots. The mole ruined the neat straight rows of the garden.

Stefan clipped the red jumper cables onto the dead battery. Stefan waited beside the humming engine of the helper truck. Stefan turned the key inside the silent gray sedan. Stefan drove the gray sedan out of the cold garage.

Wei outlined the sales report on a yellow legal pad. Wei typed a full draft at the quiet corner desk. Wei corrected the small errors with a slow careful read. Wei sent the finished report to the whole project team.

The icicle formed at the edge of the tin roof. The icicle thickened with every slow drip of meltwater. The icicle stretched almost down to the porch railing. The icicle snapped off and shattered on the front step.

Talia coaxed the big shaggy dog into the plastic tub. Talia lathered the big shaggy dog with gentle shampoo. Talia rinsed the big shaggy dog with warm clean water. Talia dried the big shaggy dog with an old beach towel.

The rumor started at a crowded stall in the market. The rumor spread through the narrow lanes of the town. The rumor reached the wide desk of the town mayor. The rumor collapsed under one plain public statement.

Bruno lit the charcoal inside the round kettle grill. Bruno seasoned the thick steaks with coarse salt and pepper. Bruno seared the thick steaks over the glowing coals. Bruno served the thick steaks on a warm clay platter.

The dandelion bloomed bright yellow in the sidewalk crack. The dandelion faded into a round white head of seeds. The dandelion released a cloud of seeds into a gust of wind. The dandelion stood bare on a thin green stem.

Keiko centered the wet clay on the spinning wheel. Keiko raised the wet clay into a smooth even wall. Keiko trimmed the base with a fine wire tool. Keiko lifted a finished bowl off the spinning wheel.

The moth fluttered up out of the dark garden hedge. The moth circled the bright porch lamp in wide loops. The moth bumped the warm glass with dusty wings. The moth settled at last-light on the cool window screen.

Camille pulled the white sheets from the washer drum. Camille pinned the white sheets along the garden line. Camille folded the white sheets into the wicker basket. Camille stacked the white sheets inside the linen closet.

The delivery truck rolled out of the busy depot gate. The delivery truck stopped in front of the blue corner house. The delivery truck dropped a large parcel at the front door. The delivery truck rumbled off toward the highway ramp.

Hana measured the pine boards with a folding ruler. Hana sawed the pine boards into six neat pieces. Hana nailed the six neat pieces into a small birdhouse. Hana hung the small birdhouse from a branch of the maple.

Old Marta collected the maple sap from the tapped trees. Old Marta boiled the maple sap in a wide shallow pan. Old Marta poured the thick syrup into small glass bottles. Old Marta capped the small glass bottles with tight lids.

The skateboard rolled away down the sloped driveway. The skateboard picked up speed past the parked cars. The skateboard hit the curb at the bottom of the hill. The skateboard flipped into the neighbor's flower bed.

Viktor tightened the loose string on the old guitar. Viktor plucked every string beside a humming tuning fork. Viktor adjusted the worn pegs until the notes rang true. Viktor played a soft slow song on the old guitar.

The pothole opened in the cracked asphalt near the bakery. The pothole widened under the wheels of heavy trucks. The pothole swallowed a hubcap from a passing van. The pothole received a bright orange cone from the city crew.

Layla rinsed the smooth gravel in a plastic pail. Layla spread the smooth gravel across the tank bottom. Layla filled the glass tank with cool clear water. Layla released two goldfish into the glass tank.

The sand dune shifted under the steady onshore wind. The sand dune crept over the old wooden rail fence. The sand dune buried the base of the faded warning sign. The sand dune settled around a bleached driftwood log.

Jonah set the small telescope on the flat rooftop. Jonah aimed the small telescope at a bright steady point. Jonah focused the blurry disk into sharp golden rings. Jonah sketched the ringed planet in a worn notebook.

The ripe peach fell from the top of the fruit crate. The ripe peach split open on the hard cobblestones. The ripe peach leaked sweet juice between the smooth stones. The ripe peach drew a busy line of tiny ants.

Petra signed a short note inside every folded card. Petra addressed the small envelopes in neat blue ink. Petra stamped the small envelopes at the kitchen table. Petra mailed the small envelopes at the corner box.

The soap bar slipped from the wet edge of the sink. The soap bar skidded across the smooth tile floor. The soap bar came to rest beneath the warm radiator. The soap bar collected a fine gray coat of dust and lint.

Anders scraped the deep snow off the front steps. Anders cleared a narrow path down to the mailbox. Anders spread rock salt along the narrow path. Anders leaned the snow shovel against the porch rail.

The tea bag dropped into the cup of steaming water. The tea bag turned the steaming water a deep amber. The tea bag released a warm scent of bergamot into the kitchen. The tea bag ended up in the small compost pail.

Meilin eased the root ball out of the cracked clay pot. Meilin loosened the tangled roots with gentle fingers. Meilin settled the leafy fern into a larger glazed pot. Meilin watered the leafy fern on the sunny windowsill.

The traffic light flickered above the quiet crossing. The traffic light froze on red without any warning. The traffic light backed up cars along both narrow streets. The traffic light went dark after one weak flicker.

Selim leaned the tall ladder against the apple tree. Selim picked the red apples from the upper branches. Selim filled the wooden crates with the red apples. Selim hauled the wooden crates down to the farm stand.

The water stain appeared on the bedroom ceiling. The water stain spread outward in a wide brown ring. The water stain revealed a slow leak beneath the shingles. The water stain guided the roofer straight to the cracked flashing.

Farmer Bell cut the ripe wheat with the red combine. Farmer Bell poured the golden grain into the tall silo. Farmer Bell baled the leftover straw across the wide field. Farmer Bell hauled the golden grain to the county mill.

The suitcase slid down the loading chute at the airport. The suitcase missed the cart for the correct flight. The suitcase flew to a distant city by mistake. The suitcase returned home in the back of a courier van.
The brown egg trembled in the warm straw nest. The brown egg cracked along a thin white line. The brown egg split open under a tiny tapping beak. The brown egg lay empty beside a damp new chick.

The tadpole hatched from a jelly egg in the still pond. The tadpole grew two strong kicking back legs. The tadpole lost the long swimming tail bit by bit. The tadpole hopped onto a wet lily pad at the pond edge.

Zoe wobbled down the driveway on the small blue bike. Zoe found a steady balance past the garden hedge. Zoe pedaled a full loop around the empty parking lot. Zoe braked to a smooth stop beside the front gate.
noncausal_texts: The town library lent a heavy atlas to a young student. The town library hosted a small chess tournament in the main hall. The town library displayed old photographs near the marble staircase. The town library received a donated wooden shelf from a volunteer.

The corner bakery sold a tray of almond croissants to a tour group. The corner bakery hung a hand-painted sign above the flour bins. The corner bakery sponsored a pie contest at the county fair. The corner bakery repaired a squeaky hinge on the oven door.

The village school planted a maple sapling near the bike racks. The village school borrowed a telescope from a science museum. The village school painted a mural of local birds on the gym wall. The village school welcomed a retired pilot for a career talk.

Mr. Okafor donated a box of paperbacks to the church sale. Mr. Okafor photographed a gray heron at the reservoir. Mr. Okafor repaired a wobbly bench outside the barbershop. Mr. Okafor entered a chili contest at the fire hall.

The city museum cleaned a bronze statue in the sculpture court. The city museum hosted a lecture on desert insects. The city museum acquired a woven quilt from a mountain village. The city museum replaced the worn carpet in the map room.

The fishing club organized a cleanup along the north jetty. The fishing club raffled a handmade canoe at the annual banquet. The fishing club printed fresh membership cards with a trout logo. The fishing club loaned a set of rods to the local scouts.

Aunt Bea knitted warm mittens for the neighbor twins. Aunt Bea taught a card game to the friendly mail carrier. Aunt Bea painted the porch swing a cheerful shade of blue. Aunt Bea found a silver thimble under the hallway radiator.

The train station displayed a model railway in the ticket hall. The train station leased a small kiosk to a coffee roaster. The train station tested the fire alarm with a loud drill. The train station unveiled a plaque for a famous conductor.

The rooftop garden attracted a pair of finches with a seed tray. The rooftop garden supplied fresh basil to a nearby bistro. The rooftop garden lost a wooden trellis in a gusty squall. The rooftop garden appeared in a photo essay in a design magazine.

Captain Reyes mapped a shifting sandbar near the lighthouse. Captain Reyes taught a knot class at the busy marina. Captain Reyes repainted the hull of a small dinghy. Captain Reyes judged a chowder contest on the crowded pier.

The gray cat knocked a pencil off the cluttered desk. The gray cat napped inside an empty terracotta flowerpot. The gray cat chased a pale moth around the floor lamp. The gray cat ignored a brand-new toy from the pet store.

The hardware store stocked a rack of copper garden bells. The hardware store sharpened a set of hedge shears for a landscaper. The hardware store gave a ladder-safety demonstration in the parking lot. The hardware store fixed a flickering light above the paint aisle.

The hiking club marked a muddy detour with orange ribbons. The hiking club published a pocket guide to local wildflowers. The hiking club rebuilt a footbridge over a rocky creek. The hiking club shared a potluck dinner at the trailhead pavilion.

Professor Lindqvist mailed a rare fern sample to a colleague overseas. Professor Lindqvist judged a poster session at the student fair. Professor Lindqvist repaired the department's ancient slide projector. Professor Lindqvist recited a short poem at the faculty dinner.

The fire station polished the antique brass bell by the entrance. The fire station adopted a spotted dog from the animal shelter. The fire station held an open house for curious schoolchildren. The fire station raised a new flag on the front pole.

The farmers market featured a fiddler near the honey stalls. The farmers market added a shaded bike corral by the fence. The farmers market collected canned goods for the food pantry. The farmers market printed cloth bags with a sunflower design.

The old mill hosted a watercolor class beside the flume. The old mill gained a fresh coat of red paint on the doors. The old mill appeared on the cover of a regional travel brochure. The old mill lent the giant grindstone to a history exhibit.

Nurse Adaeze organized a blood drive at the community hall. Nurse Adaeze grew prize tomatoes in a backyard plot. Nurse Adaeze sang alto in a concert at the bandshell. Nurse Adaeze fixed a flat bicycle tire for a coworker.

The toy shop unveiled a wind-up robot in the front window. The toy shop hosted a puppet show near the register. The toy shop donated a crate of jump ropes to the playground fund. The toy shop repaired a broken rocking horse for a customer.

The marching band debuted a new arrangement at the harvest parade. The marching band raised funds with a car wash by the gym. The marching band welcomed a tuba player from a rival town. The marching band polished the dented sousaphones in the band room.

The harbor welcomed a tall sailing ship from a distant port. The harbor installed a new bench near the bait shack. The harbor hosted a lantern festival along the seawall. The harbor dredged a shallow channel beside the ferry slip.

Grandma Lucia pickled a basket of green beans for the pantry. Grandma Lucia beat the whole family at dominoes on the patio. Grandma Lucia sewed a button eye onto a beloved teddy bear. Grandma Lucia spotted a hummingbird at the kitchen window.

The record shop hosted an acoustic set by a local duo. The record shop bought a crate of jazz albums from an estate sale. The record shop installed a listening booth near the back wall. The record shop printed tote bags with a turntable logo.

The scout troop assembled care packages for overseas soldiers. The scout troop cleared invasive vines from the creek bank. The scout troop earned a ribbon at the regional jamboree. The scout troop toured a working dairy barn outside town.

The lighthouse keeper baked a batch of rye bread in the little kitchen. The lighthouse keeper carved a driftwood gull for the mantel. The lighthouse keeper mailed a weather log to the coastal authority. The lighthouse keeper welcomed a documentary crew for an interview.

The community center offered a free tai chi class on the lawn. The community center replaced the cracked backboard in the gym. The community center hosted a mushroom-foraging talk in the annex. The community center collected winter coats in a donation bin.

The bookshop hosted a mystery author for a crowded signing. The bookshop adopted a sleepy orange cat as a mascot. The bookshop built a reading nook beneath the front stairs. The bookshop donated misprinted novels to the prison library.

Uncle Ferenc restored a cuckoo clock for a neighbor. Uncle Ferenc caught a huge carp at the reservoir. Uncle Ferenc taught a folk dance at the wedding reception. Uncle Ferenc planted a row of paprika peppers by the shed.

The botanical garden opened a moss exhibit near the koi pond. The botanical garden loaned a rare orchid to a university lab. The botanical garden hosted a beekeeping workshop in the glasshouse. The botanical garden installed braille labels along the herb walk.

The rowing club repaired a cracked oarlock in the boathouse. The rowing club hosted a pancake breakfast on the dock. The rowing club welcomed a coach from a coastal city. The rowing club donated an old shell to the maritime museum.

The roadside diner introduced a peach pie on the specials board. The roadside diner replaced the torn vinyl on two corner booths. The roadside diner hosted a trivia night for the bowling league. The roadside diner framed a postcard collection above the counter.

Ms. Nakamura folded a thousand paper stars for a charity gala. Ms. Nakamura won a photography prize with a picture of fog. Ms. Nakamura tutored a shy student in algebra at the library. Ms. Nakamura repaired a leaky birdbath in the courtyard.

The post office unveiled a stamp with a local wildflower design. The post office hired a cheerful clerk for the parcel window. The post office repainted the flagpole out front a bright white. The post office hosted a letter-writing day for the elementary school.

The drama troupe performed a comedy at the lakeside amphitheater. The drama troupe sewed a set of velvet capes for the costume trunk. The drama troupe auctioned a painted backdrop for charity. The drama troupe recruited a carpenter for the stage crew.

The campground added a rope swing near the swimming hole. The campground hosted a stargazing talk at the fire circle. The campground repaired the pump handle at the north well. The campground printed trail maps on waterproof paper.

The beekeeper sold a case of wildflower honey at the fair. The beekeeper gave a hive tour to a curious school group. The beekeeper painted the wooden hive boxes a soft green. The beekeeper wrote an article on drought for a farm journal.

The ice rink hosted a costume skate for the whole town. The ice rink installed brighter lamps above the far corner. The ice rink hired a retired figure skater as an instructor. The ice rink donated worn rental skates to a youth charity.

The radio station interviewed a balloonist about a mountain crossing. The radio station gave away concert tickets during the noon show. The radio station upgraded the old microphone in the main booth. The radio station broadcast a spelling bee from the fairgrounds.

Dr. Mensah published a paper on tide pools in a coastal journal. Dr. Mensah coached a youth soccer team at the rec field. Dr. Mensah restored an antique microscope for the clinic lobby. Dr. Mensah baked a lemon cake for the office potluck.

The garden club planted tulip bulbs around the war memorial. The garden club swapped heirloom seeds at a table in the plaza. The garden club invited a botanist to speak about ferns. The garden club raffled a wheelbarrow at the flower show.

The ferry terminal opened a small oyster bar by the waiting room. The ferry terminal installed a mural of migrating whales. The ferry terminal hosted a craft market on the covered walkway. The ferry terminal replaced the faded schedule board with a bright screen.

The tailor hemmed a velvet gown for a retired opera singer. The tailor displayed a pincushion collection in the front window. The tailor donated fabric scraps to the quilting circle. The tailor sponsored a youth cricket team in the summer league.

The bowling alley hosted a glow-in-the-dark league night. The bowling alley resurfaced two warped lanes near the snack bar. The bowling alley framed a perfect-game scorecard by the entrance. The bowling alley donated old pins to an art teacher.

Coach Petrova organized a free clinic for young sprinters. Coach Petrova repaired the sagging net on the practice court. Coach Petrova spoke about teamwork at a school assembly. Coach Petrova grew a bumper crop of cucumbers behind the garage.

The animal shelter hosted an adoption day at the plaza fountain. The animal shelter built a shaded run along the side fence. The animal shelter received a truckload of blankets from a knitting group. The animal shelter featured a three-legged terrier in a calendar.

The cycling club painted safety arrows along the canal path. The cycling club hosted a repair workshop under the bridge. The cycling club welcomed a member from a distant island town. The cycling club raffled a vintage frame at the street fair.

The windmill gained a fresh set of canvas sails from a restorer. The windmill appeared in a landscape painting at the gallery. The windmill hosted a bread-making demonstration in the base room. The windmill drew a busload of photographers at dawn.

Old Tomas carved a chess set from cherry wood for the cafe. Old Tomas won a ribbon for gooseberry jam at the fair. Old Tomas repaired the church weathervane with a borrowed ladder. Old Tomas told fishing stories to the kids at the dock.

The aquarium welcomed a rescued sea turtle to the big tank. The aquarium hosted a sleepover for a marine science class. The aquarium unveiled a jellyfish room with soft blue lights. The aquarium printed a coloring book about kelp forests.

The quilting circle stitched a starry banner for the harvest dance. The quilting circle donated lap blankets to the veterans home. The quilting circle welcomed a new member from the coast. The quilting circle displayed a patchwork map at the town hall.

The gas station installed a rack of local honey by the register. The gas station sponsored a stock car at the dirt track. The gas station repainted the faded canopy a deep green. The gas station gave free air checks during a safety weekend.

Ranger Dupont tagged a young elk near the northern meadow. Ranger Dupont led a wildflower walk for a garden society. Ranger Dupont repaired a storm-damaged sign at the overlook. Ranger Dupont recorded owl calls for a university archive.

The old theater screened a silent film with live piano. The old theater restored the velvet curtains with donated funds. The old theater hosted a yo-yo championship on the main stage. The old theater sold commemorative posters in the lobby.

The debate team hosted a public forum on the library lawn. The debate team designed a banner with a quill emblem. The debate team traveled to a tournament in a borrowed van. The debate team tutored middle schoolers in public speaking.

The dairy farm bottled a batch of chocolate milk for the fair. The dairy farm hosted a cheese-tasting evening in the barn. The dairy farm welcomed a veterinary student for a residency. The dairy farm repaired the weathercock on the silo roof.

Mrs. Adler translated a bundle of old letters for the archive. Mrs. Adler baked poppyseed rolls for the block party. Mrs. Adler photographed frost patterns on the parlor window. Mrs. Adler taught a granddaughter to play cribbage on the porch.

The arcade restored a pinball machine with a rocket theme. The arcade hosted a high-score contest for charity. The arcade installed a change machine near the skee-ball lanes. The arcade gave free tokens to the little league champions.

The birdwatching club spotted a rare warbler at the marsh boardwalk. The birdwatching club donated binoculars to the nature center. The birdwatching club published a checklist of shorebirds. The birdwatching club hosted a dawn chorus walk at the point.

The hillside chapel rang the bells for a silver anniversary. The hillside chapel hosted a harp recital at candlelight. The hillside chapel repaired a stained-glass panel of a dove. The hillside chapel welcomed a walking group for a rest stop.

The blacksmith forged a garden gate with a vine pattern. The blacksmith demonstrated nail-making at the heritage fair. The blacksmith repaired a broken plow for a hill farmer. The blacksmith donated iron hooks to the harbor museum.

The zoo welcomed a pair of red pandas from a partner park. The zoo hosted a drawing contest at the flamingo lagoon. The zoo installed a misting station along the shaded path. The zoo published a cookbook of recipes from the staff canteen.

The shepherd carved a whistle from an elder branch. The shepherd sold three fleeces at the wool market. The shepherd repaired a stone wall on the high pasture. The shepherd taught a border collie a gentle new command.

Grandpa Chen flew a dragon kite at the river festival. Grandpa Chen brewed a pot of chrysanthemum tea for the mahjong group. Grandpa Chen repaired a garden lantern with copper wire. Grandpa Chen recited an old poem at the family reunion.

The town hall unveiled a mosaic of the founding families. The town hall hosted a pancake breakfast for the fire brigade. The town hall replaced the creaky doors of the council chamber. The town hall displayed a whaling-era logbook in the lobby.

The volleyball team hosted a beach clinic for beginners. The volleyball team designed jerseys with a pelican mascot. The volleyball team volunteered at the food bank warehouse. The volleyball team repaired the sagging net at the public court.

The observatory hosted a lecture on meteor showers. The observatory polished the great mirror in the main dome. The observatory welcomed a class of blind students for a tactile tour. The observatory published a booklet on light pollution.

The chess club hosted a blindfold exhibition at the cafe. The chess club painted a giant board on the plaza bricks. The chess club welcomed a grandmaster for a simultaneous display. The chess club donated travel sets to the children's hospital.
